label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if following these steps will bring me back to the starting point. Let me break it down step by step. First, the instructions say to always face forward. So my direction doesn't change; I'm just moving in relation to that forward direction. Starting at the origin point (0,0), facing forward. 1. Take 10 steps backward. Since I'm facing forward, backward would be in the negative direction along the y-axis (assuming forward is up). So moving from (0,0) to (0, -10). 2. Take 7 steps backward. Again, moving further back along the y-axis. From (0, -10) to (0, -17). 3. Take 8 steps right. Right would be along the positive x-axis. From (0, -17) to (8, -17). 4. Take 6 steps right. More steps right. From (8, -17) to (14, -17). 5. Take 3 steps left. Left is the negative x-axis. From (14, -17) to (11, -17). Now, let's check the final position. The x-coordinate is 11, and the y-coordinate is -17. To return to the starting point (0,0), both x and y need to be 0. Here, x is 11 and y is -17. So the final position is (11, -17), which is not the starting point. Wait, but maybe I made a mistake in the direction of the steps. Let me double-check. - Backward is moving in the negative y-axis direction (assuming forward is positive y).
